title: Optional semantics use a gold question mark and readable nullopt word
summary: Optional and expected question marks use one bold gold semantic accent, while std::nullopt retains its word spelling in production.
details: The rule applies consistently in raw signatures, flipped parameters, structured declarations, return types, nested types, and expressions.
The exact standard sentinel std::nullopt retains its readable word spelling, as do custom::nullopt, longer identifiers, strings, comments, and include paths.
Cursor and Visual reveal continue to show original source spelling, and the presentation never changes buffer bytes or source-row count.
Gold is #e0af68 in the active palette and the semantic marker is bold.

The owner submitted the gold-empty-set candidate under catalog hash 0a4f7add82c312c0b3ffaf6e58a9904856b8c9a6ef492be01ef25ad6239627d1 with an explicit note selecting gold but rejecting the glyph; the resolved profile therefore combines gold with the word spelling.
rationale: The former dim or path-dependent question mark read like comment noise instead of part of the type grammar.
Gold makes absence conspicuous without borrowing mutation red or library provenance colors.
The nullopt word remains legible and typographically stable in the owner's active font.
alternatives: Keep the mixed neutral marker; rejected because the semantic punctuation was too subdued and inconsistent.
Use ∅ for std::nullopt; rejected after real-grid comparison because the glyph was ugly in the owner's font.
Use cyan or violet for the semantic marker; visually valid but not selected.

provenance.note: Owner selected gold and explicitly rejected the empty-set glyph after comparing real embedded-Neovim captures.

provenance.reference: lua/custom/dans_frontend_cpp/style.lua, render.lua, pointer.lua, aliases.lua, and highlights.lua
provenance.note: The shared buffer-local profile and raw/structured rendering paths implement the selected spelling and accent.

evidence.claim: Real ext_linegrid RGB runs pin the gold bold marker, word and ∅ alternatives, exact sentinel boundaries, and source integrity.
Recursive type positions, expressions, cursor/Visual reveal, row identity, and exact-namespace negatives are pinned.

questions: What color is the optional question mark?

last_verified.method: Owner's conditional selection note normalized to gold-word, then verified through RGB, interaction, source-integrity, font-feedback, and exact-boundary assertions.
